title: Ending.
content:
That is solemn we have ended, --
    Be it but a play,
    Or a glee among the garrets,
    Or a holiday,

    Or a leaving home; or later,
    Parting with a world
    We have understood, for better
    Still it be unfurled.
meaning:
"Ending." is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
